channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Plateau Pastor Claims US Troops Deployment Skipped Christian Areas, Blames NSA Ribadu
item.description: Rev. Ezekiel Dachomo, wey be Plateau State cleric and also the Barkin Ladi Regional Chairman for Church of Christ in Nations, don come out to accuse National Security Adviser Nuhu Ribadu say e dey div...

item.title: Tanzania and Uganda strengthen cooperation on SGR, trade, and energy projects
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania and Uganda have officially signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to enhance cooperation in several important areas. The MoU focuses mainly on the development of the Isaka...

item.title: Persons deprived of liberty participate in Antipolo’s 2nd district special elections
item.description: Inmates, or persons deprived of liberty (PDLs), in Antipolo City were given the chance to vote on Saturday, March 14, during the special elections for the city’s second congressional district. Accordi...

item.title: South Africa and Eswatini Formalise Revised Komati Basin Water Agreement
item.description: South Africa and Eswatini are set to formally sign a revised treaty on the development and management of the Komati Basin's water resources at Maguga Dam in Eswatini on 13 March 2026. The signing invo...

item.title: Mexico explores teen social media restrictions inspired by Australian model
item.description: Mexico is exploring the possibility of introducing restrictions on social media use for teenagers, taking inspiration from Australia’s recent policy. The Mexican Public Education Secretary, Mario Delg...

item.title: Six Terrorists Killed in Intelligence-Based CTD Operation in Lakki Marwat
item.description: In Lakki Marwat, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, an intelligence-led operation conducted by the Counter-Terrorism Department (CTD) resulted in the deaths of six terrorists. The incident occurred in the Shagayi ar...

item.title: Grace Tame on activism, public backlash, and standing for human rights
item.description: In this exclusive piece for Crikey, Grace Tame reflects on the political and social backlash she faced after being labelled 'difficult' by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and the controversy that foll...

item.title: Weekly Political Cartoons Reflect America's Political Climate
item.description: Each week, political cartoonists from across the United States and from a wide range of political leanings gather their creative energy to capture the ongoing drama of American politics. Their drawing...

item.title: The Influence of Irish Immigrants on Pittsburgh’s Culture, Labor, and Community
item.description: Pittsburgh has a deep-rooted Irish heritage that continues to shape the city’s culture, neighborhoods, and institutions. Irish immigrants first arrived in the 18th century, including both Scotch-Irish...

item.title: Raptors overcome Suns 122-115 with Ingram scoring 36 points
item.description: The Toronto Raptors staged a strong comeback to defeat the Phoenix Suns 122-115 on Friday night, ending the Suns’ four-game winning streak. Brandon Ingram led the way for Toronto with 36 points, suppo...

item.title: Jessie Buckley’s Hometown Prepares for Oscars as Actress Becomes Favourite for Best Actress Award
item.description: Jessie Buckley’s home town of Killarney, County Kerry, is eagerly anticipating the Oscars, where she is widely tipped to become the first Irish woman to win the award for best actress for her role in ...

item.title: How Kenyan artist Malosh chose music over an engineering career
item.description: This story focuses on Malosh, a young Kenyan artist who decided to follow his passion for music instead of pursuing a more conventional path in engineering. The article introduces him as a gifted boy ...

item.title: FKF Urged to Balance Diaspora and Local Talent Ahead of 2027 Afcon
item.description: As Kenya prepares to co-host the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon) with Uganda and Tanzania, the Football Kenya Federation (FKF) is actively courting foreign-based players with Kenyan heritage. Coach...

item.title: Trump's Post Highlights Strategic Message Behind Kharg Island Strike
item.description: The Sky News article, written by U.S. correspondent David Blevins, analyzes former President Donald Trump’s online reaction following the U.S. strike on Iran’s Kharg Island. Trump used his Truth Socia...

item.title: Assam sends first GI-certified Joha rice consignment to UK and Italy
item.description: India has achieved a notable milestone in agricultural exports by shipping its first consignment of Joha rice, which is Geographical Indication (GI) tagged, from Assam to the United Kingdom and Italy....

item.title: Unresolved 1986 Murder of Black & Decker Executive Kenneth Marston: Daughters Suspect Corporate Corruption
item.description: On 25 April 1986, Kenneth Marston, a 42-year-old British executive for Black & Decker, was shot dead outside his family's villa in a Lyon suburb by a gunman on foot who fled in a waiting car. Marston,...

item.title: German Diplomat Explores Dar es Salaam’s Historic Sites and Cultural Heritage
item.description: Maximillian Müller, the Deputy Head of Mission at the German Embassy in Tanzania, has spent eight months exploring the country and learning about its history and culture. Recently, he went on a person...

item.title: Yvette Cooper on UK Foreign Policy, Iran Conflict, and International Law
item.description: Yvette Cooper, the UK Foreign Secretary since September 2025, has been navigating a highly tense period in international affairs, particularly concerning the ongoing US-Israeli bombardment of Iran. Sh...

item.title: Absa Bank Tanzania launches ElevateHer AI Programme to train young women in artificial intelligence
item.description: Absa Bank Tanzania, in partnership with Microsoft and Women in Tech Global, has launched the ElevateHer AI Programme to help 5,000 young Tanzanian women gain essential skills in artificial intelligenc...

item.title: Tinubu Urges Media to Monitor States and Local Governments for True Federalism
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don meet with big media leaders like Newspaper Proprietors’ Association of Nigeria (NPAN), Nigerian Guild of Editors (NGE), and Broadcasting Organisations of Nigeria (BON) ...
